flavorEn: Although it’s called a guardian deity, terrible calamities sometimes befall those who recklessly approach Tapu Fini.

flavorJa: 無警戒で 好奇心が 強く 危ない 目に あうことも 多い。 ピンチになると ワープで 逃げる。
flavorEn: Cosmog is very curious but not very cautious, often placing itself in danger. If things start to look dicey, it teleports away.

flavorJa: 大気の 塵を すごい 勢いで 吸いこみ 進化の エネルギーを コアで 懸命に つくっている。
flavorEn: It sucks in dust from the air at an astounding rate, frantically building up energy within its core as preparation for evolution.

flavorJa: 太陽を 食らいし 獣 と かつて 呼ばれた。 無尽蔵の 光エネルギーを 放出する。
flavorEn: Solgaleo was once known as the Beast That Devours the Sun. Energy in the form of light radiates boundlessly from it.

flavorJa: 周囲から 光を すべて 奪い 自らは 満月の 姿となり 闇夜に 光を 放つ。
flavorEn: It steals the light from its surroundings and then becomes the full moon, showering its own light across the night sky.

flavorJa: ウルトラホールから この世界に 現れた。 ポケモンや 人間に 寄生して 生きているようだ。
flavorEn: It appeared in this world from an Ultra Wormhole. Nihilego appears to be a parasite that lives by feeding on people and Pokémon.

flavorJa: 異常な までに 膨張する 筋肉を 見せつけてきた。 ウルトラビーストの 一種。
flavorEn: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.

flavorJa: この世界では 異質で 危険だが 本来 棲んでいる 世界では 普通に 見かける 生物らしい。
flavorEn: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

flavorJa: ウルトラビーストと 呼ばれる。 樹木のように 地面に 手足を 刺したまま 動かないもの もいる。
flavorEn: They’ve been dubbed Ultra Beasts. Some of them stand unmoving, like trees, with their arms and legs stuck into the ground.

flavorJa: この世界では 異質で 危険だが 本来 棲んでいる 世界では 普通に 見かける 生物らしい。
flavorEn: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

flavorJa: この世界では 異質で 危険だが 本来 棲んでいる 世界では 普通に 見かける 生物らしい。
flavorEn: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

flavorJa: ＵＢと 呼ばれる 未知の 生命。 常に 腹を すかせているのか ずっと なにかを 喰らっている。
flavorEn: An unknown life-form called a UB. It may be constantly hungry—it is certainly always devouring something.

flavorJa: 生きるために 光を 求めて 暴れまわる。 レーザー光線を 放ち あらゆるものを 切り刻む。
flavorEn: It needs light to survive, and it goes on a rampage seeking it out. Its laser beams will cut anything to pieces.

flavorJa: およそ ５００年前 科学者に よって 作られた。 ソウルハートと 呼ばれる パーツが 本体なのだ。
flavorEn: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

flavorJa: あらゆる 影の 中に 潜りこむ 能力を もっているため 長い あいだ 誰にも 見つからなかった。
flavorEn: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.

flavorJa: 異世界に 棲む ウルトラビースト。 頭の 毒針から 毒液を 相手に かけて ケラケラ 笑う。
flavorEn: An Ultra Beast that lives in a different world, it cackles wildly as it sprays its opponents with poison from the needles on its head.

flavorJa: ウルトラビーストの 一種。 毒針から 粘度が 高く 光る 毒液を 発射する。
flavorEn: One kind of Ultra Beast, it fires a glowing, venomous liquid from its needles. This liquid is also immensely adhesive.

flavorJa: ＵＢらしき 謎の 生命体。 突然 動き出して 襲ってきた 石垣の 正体は こいつだ。
flavorEn: When stone walls started moving and attacking, the brute’s true identity was this mysterious life-form, which brings to mind an Ultra Beast.

flavorJa: ウルトラホールから 現れた ＵＢ。 爆発で 相手を 驚かし その隙に 生気を 奪っていた。
flavorEn: A UB that appeared from an Ultra Wormhole, it causes explosions, then takes advantage of opponents’ surprise to rob them of their vitality.

flavorJa: 手足の 肉球から 放電。 ゼラオラが 駆け抜けると 稲妻が 光り 雷鳴が 轟く。
flavorEn: Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.

flavorJa: 群れになって 生活するが 時が くると １匹の 強い メルタンが 仲間たちを 取りこみ 進化する。
flavorEn: They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.

flavorJa: 遠心力で 弾みを つけて 繰りだす 重い ナットの パンチは ポケモン 最強と いわれる。
flavorEn: Centrifugal force is behind the punches of Melmetal’s heavy hex-nut arms. Melmetal is said to deliver the strongest punches of all Pokémon.

flavorJa: スティックの 連打で 攻撃。 すごい スピードで 叩くうちに どんどん テンションが 上がるのだ。
flavorEn: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.

flavorJa: 激しい ビートを 刻むことに 夢中になる あまり 戦いで 相手が 気絶しても 気づかない。
flavorEn: When it’s drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won’t even notice that it’s already knocked out its opponent.

flavorJa: ドラムテクニックに 優れた ものが ボスになる。 穏やかな 気性で グループの 調和を 重んじる。
flavorEn: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

flavorJa: 戦う 準備が 整うと 鼻の 頭と 足の 裏の 肉球が 高熱を 発する。
flavorEn: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it’s raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

flavorJa: 手を 使わずに 木の 枝から 木の実を 摘み取り リフティング。 足技を 磨く 練習。
flavorEn: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.

genus: ストライカーポケモン
flavorJa: 攻守に 優れ 応援されると さらに 燃えるが スタンドプレイに 走り ピンチを 招くこともある。
flavorEn: It’s skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

flavorJa: 皮膚の 色は 濡れると 変わる。 カモフラージュ されたかの ように 姿が 見えなく なるのだ。
flavorEn: When it gets wet, its skin changes color, and this Pokémon becomes invisible as if it were camouflaged.

flavorJa: 頭が よく 面倒くさがり。 縄張りに 敵が 近づかないよう そこかしこに 罠を 仕掛けている。
flavorEn: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.

genus: エージェントポケモン
flavorJa: 指先から 放つ 水鉄砲は マッハ３の 速さ。 瞬膜で 急所を 見抜いて 撃ちぬくぞ。
flavorEn: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes’ weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

flavorJa: つねに 木の実を 食っているので 見かけ以上に タフ。 木の実を 狙って 畑に 現れる。
flavorEn: It eats berries nonstop—a habit that has made it more resilient than it looks. It’ll show up on farms, searching for yet more berries.

flavorJa: どんなに 硬い 木の実の 殻も 自慢の 歯で ボリボリ 齧る。 ガラルでは よく 見る ポケモン。
flavorEn: Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.

flavorJa: 小さい 体で 機敏に 飛んで 隙を 突く 戦法で 体の 大きな 相手を 手玉に取る。
flavorEn: Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.

flavorJa: 厳しい 戦いを くぐり抜けて 相手の 力量を 正確に 判断する 力が 身についた。
flavorEn: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent’s strength.

flavorJa: 飛行能力に 優れていて とても 賢いため ガラル地方で 空の タクシーとして 活躍。
flavorEn: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region’s airborne taxi service.

flavorJa: 畑で よく見かける ポケモン。 体に 生えた 毛で まわりで 起きていることを 感じとる。
flavorEn: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

flavorJa: 殻の 中で 成長中。 サイコパワーで 外の 様子を うかがい 進化に 備えている。
flavorEn: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

flavorJa: サイコパワーを 放ち 周囲を 調べている。 観測範囲は 周囲 １０キロにも 達するぞ。
flavorEn: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what’s around it—and what’s around it can include things over six miles away.

flavorJa: 用心深く ずる賢い。 エサを 盗むと しっぽで 足跡を 消しながら 逃げるのだ。
flavorEn: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

flavorJa: 身軽な 体と 鋭い ツメで エサや タマゴを 盗んで まわる。 パルスワンが 天敵。
flavorEn: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

flavorJa: そよ風で くるくる 回りながら 楽しそうに 歌う 愛らしさに 魅せられ 育てる 人が 多い。
flavorEn: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

flavorJa: 頭の 綿毛で つくった 糸は 光沢があり とても 美しい。 ガラル地方の 名産品。
flavorEn: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

flavorJa: 毛が 伸びすぎると 動けない。 ウールーの 体毛で 織られた 布は 驚くほど 丈夫。
flavorEn: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

flavorJa: 立派に 伸びた ツノは 異性に アピールするために 生えている。 武器として 使うことはない。
flavorEn: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

flavorJa: 頭の ツノは 岩の 硬さ。 ツノで 戦い ひるんだ 隙に パクリと 噛みつき 離さない。
flavorEn: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

flavorJa: 首が 伸び縮みする。 離れた ところから 首を 一気に 伸ばし 鋭い キバで 敵を しとめる。
flavorEn: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

flavorJa: おやつに 釣られて 人の 仕事を 手伝う 食いしん坊。 パチパチと 電気を まとって ひた走る。
flavorEn: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

flavorJa: 電気の 力で 脚力を 増強。 トップスピードは 時速 ９０キロを 軽く 超える。
flavorEn: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

flavorJa: 岩だらけの 悪路を ものともせず 一輪車のように 走りまわる。 石炭を 燃やして 生きている。
flavorEn: It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.

flavorJa: 脚を 高速で 回転させて 時速３０キロで 走る。 摂氏 １０００度の 炎を 噴き出す。
flavorEn: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

flavorJa: 戦いになると 石炭の 山が 真っ赤に 燃え上がり 火の粉を まき散らして 周囲を 焦がす。
flavorEn: While it’s engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

genus: りんごぐらしポケモン
flavorJa: 生まれると りんごに 潜りこむ。 中身を 食べながら 成長し りんごの 味が 進化を 決める。
flavorEn: As soon as it’s born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.

flavorJa: りんごの 皮の 翼で 飛んで 強酸性の 唾液を 飛ばす。 りんごの 形に 変形する。
flavorEn: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

flavorJa: 甘い 蜜で 体が おおわれ 背中の 皮は とくに 甘く 昔は 子どもの おやつだった。
flavorEn: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

flavorJa: 鼻の 穴から 砂を 噴射。 敵の 目を くらました 隙に 地中に 潜って 身を 隠す。
flavorEn: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

flavorJa: 独特の とぐろの 巻き方は 砂袋の 砂を より 効率 よく 噴射 するため なのだ。
flavorEn: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

flavorJa: 食いしん坊で エサの サシカマスを 丸飲みするが たまに 間違えて ほかの ポケモンに 食らいつく。
flavorEn: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.

flavorJa: 腹一杯で 極端に 動きが 鈍った ところを ウッウに 丸飲みに される。
flavorEn: After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.

flavorJa: 尾びれを 回転させ 一気に 突撃。 １００ノットを 超える 速度で 獲物を つらぬくぞ。
flavorEn: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

flavorJa: 毒素を 化学変化 させて 電気を 出す。 電力は 弱いが ビリビリと 痺れる。
flavorEn: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

flavorJa: ケンカ腰で 気が 短い。 よどんだ 水を がぶ飲みして 水中の 毒素を 取りこむ。
flavorEn: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

flavorJa: 発熱した 体で 獲物を 締め上げる。 こんがり 焼けたら むしゃむしゃ いただくのだ。
flavorEn: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

flavorJa: 攻撃的な 性質。 焼けた 体も 危険だが 大きな キバも 鋭いぞ。
flavorEn: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

flavorJa: ３歳児 くらいの 賢さ。 触手は よくちぎれるが 再生するので 気にしない。
flavorEn: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.

genus: じゅうじゅつポケモン
flavorJa: おのれの腕を 試すべく 陸に 上がり 対戦相手を 探す。 戦い終えると 海に 帰る。
flavorEn: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

flavorJa: すみかに している ティーカップは 有名な アンティークの 食器。 多くの 贋作が 出回っている。
flavorEn: The teacup in which this Pokémon makes its home is a famous piece of antique tableware. Many forgeries are in circulation.

flavorJa: 残された 紅茶を 見かけると 自分の 体を 注ぎこむ。 その 紅茶は ポットデスになる。
flavorEn: Leaving leftover black tea unattended is asking for this Pokémon to come along and pour itself into it, turning the tea into a new Polteageist.

flavorJa: 人気の ない 場所が 好き。 強い 感情を 感じとると 一目散に 逃げ出してしまう。
flavorEn: If this Pokémon senses a strong emotion, it will run away as fast as it can. It prefers areas without people.

flavorJa: 頭の房で 相手を 殴り 黙らせる。 プロボクサーさえ 一発 ＫＯの 破壊力。
flavorEn: Using the braids on its head, it pummels foes to get them to quiet down. One blow from those braids would knock out a professional boxer.

flavorJa: 別名は 森の 魔女。 騒ぎたてると 触手のツメで 引き裂かれるかも しれないぞ。
flavorEn: If you’re too loud around it, you risk being torn apart by the claws on its tentacle. This Pokémon is also known as the Forest Witch.

flavorJa: 民家に 忍びこみ 盗みを 働き さらに 悔しがる 人の マイナスエネルギーも いただくのだ。
flavorEn: It sneaks into people’s homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.

flavorJa: 悪知恵を 使って 夜の 森に 誘い込もうとする。 農作物を 育てる 力を もつらしい。
flavorEn: With sly cunning, it tries to lure people into the woods. Some believe it to have the power to make crops grow.

genus: ビルドアップポケモン
flavorJa: 髪の毛は 筋肉繊維の ように 働き ほどけると 触手の 動きで 相手を 絡めとる。
flavorEn: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.

flavorJa: ケンカを 繰り返し 進化。 腕をクロスし 叫ぶ 雄叫びは どんな 相手も 怯ませるぞ。
flavorEn: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

flavorJa: 戦いに 明け暮れて 進化した 結果 伸ばすと 短剣に 変わる 物騒な ツメを 手に入れた。
flavorEn: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

flavorJa: 魂を 被う 霊体の 体には 注意。 触れると 石のように 動けなく なるぞ。
flavorEn: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You’ll become stiff as stone if you touch it.

flavorJa: 硬いネギの 葉で 攻撃を いなし 鋭い 茎で 反撃。 ネギは 武具であり 食材。
flavorEn: After deflecting attacks with its hard leaf shield, it strikes back with its sharp leek stalk. The leek stalk is both weapon and food.

genus: コメディアンポケモン
flavorJa: ユーモラスな 動きで みんなの 人気者。 お腹の 模様から サイコパワーを 放出する。
flavorEn: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

flavorJa: 影の ような 体に 触れては いけない。 絵に 刻まれた 恐ろしい 記憶を 見せられるぞ。
flavorEn: Never touch its shadowlike body, or you’ll be shown the horrific memories behind the picture carved into it.

flavorJa: マホミルが 姿を みせた パティスリーは 大繁盛が 約束されると いわれている。
flavorEn: They say that any patisserie visited by Milcery is guaranteed success and good fortune.

flavorJa: 手から 生みだす クリームは マホイップが 幸せなとき 甘味と コクが 深まる。
flavorEn: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

flavorJa: ６匹で １匹の ポケモン。 隊列を 組み替えながら チームワークで 戦うのだ。
flavorEn: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

flavorJa: 棘の １本 １本に 電気を 蓄える。 折れた 棘でも ３時間は 放電を 続けるぞ。
flavorEn: It stores electricity in each spine. Even if one gets broken off, it still continues to emit electricity for at least three hours.

flavorJa: 地面に 積もった 雪を 食べる。 たくさん 食べれば 食べるほど 背中の 棘は 立派に 育つ。
flavorEn: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.

flavorJa: 野山を 荒らすものには 容赦 しない。 冷たいはねで 飛びまわり 吹雪を 起こして 懲らしめる。
flavorEn: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

flavorJa: １年に 一度 きまった 日時に どこから ともなく 集まり 輪に なって 並ぶ 習性が ある。
flavorEn: Once a year, on a specific date and at a specific time, they gather out of nowhere and form up in a circle.

flavorJa: 暑さに 弱い 顔を いつも 氷で 冷やしている。 頭の 毛を 海に たらして 餌を釣る。
flavorEn: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.

flavorJa: ツノで 近くの 生き物の 気持ちを 感じとる。 ポジティブな 感情が 力の 源。
flavorEn: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.

flavorJa: どんなに 食べても すぐに お腹が すく。 電気で ローストした タネを 大切に 持ち歩く。
flavorEn: It carries electrically roasted seeds with it as if they’re precious treasures. No matter how much it eats, it always gets hungry again in short order.

flavorJa: 力仕事なら お任せ。 銅の 体は 雨で 錆び 鮮やかな 緑に 変わる。
flavorEn: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

flavorJa: 群れを つくって 暮らしている。 鼻の 握力は 大岩も 粉々に 砕くほど 強い。
flavorEn: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

flavorJa: しっぽの たくましい 筋肉で 発電する。 下半身に くらべ 上半身が 小さすぎる。
flavorEn: The powerful muscles in its tail generate its electricity. Compared to its lower body, its upper half is entirely too small.

flavorJa: 古代の 海辺で 暮らし 餌を 体の 氷で 保存できたが 動きが 鈍いため 絶滅した。
flavorEn: This Pokémon lived on prehistoric seashores and was able to preserve food with the ice on its body. It went extinct because it moved so slowly.

flavorJa: 時速６０キロを 超える 自慢の 脚力で 走れるが 水中でしか 呼吸できない。
flavorEn: Its mighty legs are capable of running at speeds exceeding 40 mph, but this Pokémon can’t breathe unless it’s underwater.

flavorJa: どんな 攻撃にも 傷つかない 面の皮を もつが 呼吸が 不自由だったため 絶滅した。
flavorEn: The skin on its face is impervious to attack, but breathing difficulties made this Pokémon go extinct anyway.

flavorJa: 特殊な 金属の 体は 軽く 身のこなしは 抜群。 雨が 苦手で 洞窟に 棲む。
flavorEn: The special metal that composes its body is very light, so this Pokémon has considerable agility. It lives in caves because it dislikes the rain.

flavorJa: １匹では 子どもにも 負けるくらい 非力だが 仲間の 協力で 鍛えられ 進化して 強くなる。
flavorEn: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.

flavorJa: 世話をする ドラメシヤを 頭に 乗せていないと 落ち着かないので ほかの ポケモンを 乗せようとする。
flavorEn: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it’ll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.

flavorJa: ツノに 入った ドラメシヤは マッハの スピードで 飛ばされるのを 心待ちに しているらしい。
flavorEn: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult’s horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.

flavorJa: 長い 眠りに ついていた ザマゼンタの 姉 とも ライバルとも いわれる ポケモン。
flavorEn: This Pokémon has slumbered for many years. Some say it’s Zamazenta’s elder sister—others say the two Pokémon are rivals.

flavorJa: 石像の ような 姿で 忘れ去られるほどの あいだ 眠りに ついていた ポケモン。
flavorEn: This Pokémon slept for aeons while in the form of a statue. It was asleep for so long, people forgot that it ever existed.

flavorJa: ２万年前に 落ちた 隕石の 中に いた。 ダイマックスの 謎に 関係している らしい。
flavorEn: It was inside a meteorite that fell 20,000 years ago. There seems to be a connection between this Pokémon and the Dynamax phenomenon.

flavorJa: 頭の 白く 長い 体毛を 引っぱると 気合が 高まり 丹田から パワーが 湧きあがる。
flavorEn: If Kubfu pulls the long white hair on its head, its fighting spirit heightens and power wells up from the depths of its belly.

flavorJa: 遠い 地方の 山岳地帯で 暮らす。 断崖絶壁を 走り 足腰を 鍛え 技を 磨く。
flavorEn: Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves.

flavorJa: 体に 生える ツルは ちぎれると 土の 栄養分となって 森の 植物たちを 育てるのだ。
flavorEn: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

genus: エレクトロンポケモン
flavorJa: 全身が 電気エネルギーを 作る 器官。 ガラル全土の 電気を 作りだすことができる。
flavorEn: Its entire body is made up of a single organ that generates electrical energy. Regieleki is capable of creating all Galar’s electricity.

genus: りゅうぎょくポケモン
flavorJa: 全身が ドラゴンエネルギーの 結晶。 すべての ドラゴン ポケモンの 力を 持つらしい。
flavorEn: Its body is composed of crystallized dragon energy. Regidrago is said to have the powers of every dragon Pokémon.

flavorJa: 凄まじい 怪力の 持ち主。 顔を 覆う 氷の マスクは ダイヤモンドの １００倍 硬い。
flavorEn: Glastrier has tremendous physical strength, and the mask of ice covering its face is 100 times harder than diamond.

flavorJa: 闇夜を 駆けぬけ 寝ているものの 生命エネルギーを 吸い取る。 孤独と 静寂を 好む。
flavorEn: As it dashes through the night, Spectrier absorbs the life-force of sleeping creatures. It craves silence and solitude.

flavorJa: 大昔の ガラルを 統べていた 伝説の 王。 心を 癒し 草木を 芽吹かせる 力がある。
flavorEn: Calyrex is known in legend as a king that ruled over Galar in ancient times. It has the power to cause hearts to mend and plants to spring forth.
The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.
A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.
I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.
Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

genus: クライミングポケモン
flavorEn: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.
Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname ”sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.
When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.
The sweet scent its body gives off mesmerizes those around it. The scent grows stronger when this Pokémon is in the sun.
The hardness of Floragato’s fur depends on the Pokémon’s mood. When Floragato is prepared to battle, its fur becomes pointed and needle sharp.
With skillful misdirection, it rigs foes with pollen-packed flower bombs. Meowscarada sets off the bombs before its foes realize what’s going on.
Its flame sac is small, so energy is always leaking out. This energy is released from the dent atop Fuecoco’s head and flickers to and fro.
The valve in Crocalor’s flame sac is closely connected to its vocal cords. This Pokémon utters a guttural cry as it spews flames every which way.
Skeledirge’s gentle singing soothes the souls of all that hear it. It burns its enemies to a crisp with flames of over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Its strong legs let it easily swim around in even fast-flowing rivers. It likes to keep things tidy and is prone to overthinking things.
The hardworking Quaxwell observes people and Pokémon from various regions and incorporates their movements into its own dance routines.
Dancing in ways that evoke far-away places, this Pokémon mesmerizes all that see it. Flourishes of its decorative water feathers slice into its foes.
This Pokémon spurns all but the finest of foods. Its body gives off an herblike scent that bug Pokémon detest.
It entrances female Pokémon with the sweet, alluring scent that wafts from all over its body.
The thread it secretes from its rear is as strong as wire. The secret behind the thread’s strength is the topic of ongoing research.
Spidops covers its territory in tough, sticky threads to set up traps for intruders.
It’s highly skilled at a fighting style in which it uses its jumping capabilities to dodge incoming attacks while also dealing damage to opponents.
It uses its normally folded third set of legs when in Showdown Mode. This places a huge burden on its body, so it can’t stay in this mode for long.
The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.
Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.
Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.
The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.
The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.
The yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.
The surface of this Pokémon’s skin hardens when exposed to intense heat, and its body has an appetizing aroma.
This Pokémon converts nutrients into oil, which it stores in the fruit on its head. It can easily go a whole week without eating or drinking.
It basks in the sun to its heart’s content until the fruits on its head ripen. After that, Dolliv departs from human settlements and goes on a journey.
This Pokémon drives back enemies by launching its rich, aromatic oil at them with enough force to smash a boulder.
Green-feathered flocks hold the most sway. When they’re out searching for food in the mornings and evenings, it gets very noisy.
The ground scrapes its body as it travels, causing it to leave salt behind. Salt is constantly being created and replenished inside Nacli’s body.
It compresses rock salt inside its body and shoots out hardened salt pellets with enough force to perforate an iron sheet.
Many Pokémon gather around Garganacl, hoping to lick at its mineral-rich salt.
Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.
This Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs.
An old set of armor steeped in grudges caused this Pokémon’s evolution. Ceruledge cuts its enemies to pieces without mercy.
It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.
What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt’s belly-button dynamo.
These Pokémon make their nests on coastal cliffs. The nests have a strange, crackling texture, and they’re a popular delicacy.
It uses its throat sac to store electricity generated by its wings. There’s hardly any oil in its feathers, so it is a poor swimmer.
Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.
Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.
To keep enemies away from its territory, it paints markings around its nest using a poisonous liquid that has an acrid odor.
Each Grafaiai paints its own individual pattern, and it will paint that same pattern over and over again throughout its life.
Not even Bramblin knows where it is headed as it tumbles across the wilderness, blown by the wind. It loathes getting wet.
Brambleghast wanders around arid regions. On rare occasions, mass outbreaks of these Pokémon will bury an entire town.
Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.
It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.
This Pokémon lives on sheer cliffs. It sidesteps opponents’ attacks, then lunges for their weak spots with its claws.
Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.
The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.
It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.
An infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.
It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.
It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.
This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it’s satisfied with the result.
These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other’s hammers by smashing them together.
The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.
Though it looks like Diglett, Wiglett is an entirely different species. The resemblance seems to be a coincidental result of environmental adaptation.
A variety of fish Pokémon, Wugtrio was once considered to be a regional form of Dugtrio.
Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.
Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.
Its physical capabilities are no different than a Finizen’s, but when its allies are in danger, it transforms and powers itself up.
The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.
Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.
It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider’s body heat warms Cyclizar’s back and lifts the Pokémon’s spirit.
This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It maintains its metal body by consuming iron from the soil.
Glimmet’s toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.
Glimmora’s petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.
This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.
A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.
Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as “synchronizing,” an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.
It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.
Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.
Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.
It treats Tatsugiri like its boss and follows it loyally. Though powerful, Dondozo is apparently not very smart.
Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.
It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.
It lives at the bottom of ponds and swamps. It will carry Wooper on its back and ferry them across water from one shore to the other.
The hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.
It drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.
Though it commands a massive army in battle, it’s not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.
This creature resembles a mysterious Pokémon that, according to a paranormal magazine, has lived since ancient times.
It resembles a mysterious Pokémon described in a paranormal magazine as a Jigglypuff from one billion years ago.
It bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.
It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.
This Pokémon somewhat resembles an ancient form of Volcarona that was introduced in a dubious magazine.
It slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.
Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.
It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.
This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.
It’s possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.
No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with an object described in an old book.
Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.
This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.
It attacks with the blade of its frozen dorsal fin by doing a front flip in the air. Arctibax’s strong back and legs allow it to pull off this technique.
It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.
It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it’s actually a Pokémon.
It has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.
It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.
The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.
It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.
The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.
According to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to a phenomenon that occurs in a certain region.
It’s possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.
This Pokémon resembles Cyclizar, but it is far burlier and more ferocious. Nothing is known about its ecology or other features.
This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.
It resembles an illustration published in a paranormal magazine, said to be a depiction of a super-ancient Suicune.
According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.
The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.
Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.
It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home's floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.
Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.
Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.
Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.
This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.
It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.

genus: りんごオロチポケモン
flavorEn: These capricious syrpents have banded together. On the rare occasion that their moods align, their true power is unleashed.

genus: パラドックスポケモン
flavorEn: It resembles an eerie Pokémon once shown in a paranormal magazine. That Pokémon was said to be an Entei regenerated from a fossil.

genus: パラドックスポケモン
flavorEn: It bears resemblance to a Pokémon that became a hot topic for a short while after a paranormal magazine touted it as Raikou's ancestor.

genus: パラドックスポケモン
flavorEn: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

genus: パラドックスポケモン
flavorEn: There was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.
It’s thought that this Pokémon lived in ancient Paldea until it got caught in seismic shifts and went extinct.
Its peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.
